name: Knowledge Graph RAG
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: GPT-4o for graph-aware response generation, entity extraction, and relationship inference

enterprise.note: Provisioned throughput for high-volume graph reasoning

services.name: Azure Cosmos DB (Gremlin API)
services.purpose: Knowledge graph store — entities as vertices, relationships as edges, Gremlin traversal queries

services.name: Azure AI Search
services.purpose: Vector + keyword search over document chunks — initial retrieval before graph augmentation

services.name: Container Apps
services.purpose: GraphRAG API — query routing, graph traversal orchestration, context assembly

services.name: Azure Functions
services.purpose: Graph ingestion pipeline — entity extraction, relationship mapping, index synchronization

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: Cosmos DB keys, AI Search admin keys, OpenAI API keys

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Graph traversal latency, retrieval quality, entity resolution accuracy, token usage

services.name: Log Analytics
services.purpose: Centralized logging — graph operations, ingestion pipeline runs, query diagnostics

optimization_tips: Use GPT-4o-mini for entity extraction and relationship inference — reserve GPT-4o for final response generation only
Cache frequent graph traversal patterns — common 2-hop queries can be precomputed and stored as materialized views
Batch entity extraction during document ingestion — process 10-20 documents per LLM call
Use Cosmos DB serverless for dev — saves $150+/month vs provisioned for low-throughput testing
Pre-filter with AI Search vector retrieval before graph traversal — avoids expensive full-graph scans
Set Gremlin query depth limits (max 3 hops) — deeper traversals are exponentially more expensive in RUs
